Earlier runs (before today's morning refresh) reported 13–14, which aligns with the current count.

glasswing_cves.notes: VulnCheck attributable CVE count holds at 40. Only CVE-2026-4747 is explicitly named as a Glasswing/Mythos autonomous discovery; the other 39 are inferred from researcher affiliation. Anthropic has signaled a fuller disclosure around July 2026.

rhel8_errata.source: Estimated / prior-run value retained (Red Hat does not publish a clean monthly aggregate; stack.watch /redhat/enterprise-linux/8.0 path still 404s — parent /redhat/enterprise-linux URL used as fallback for YTD, monthly split unchanged)

projection_comparison.commentary: April 2026 is extrapolating to ~6,082 — ~500 below the conservative May anchor (6,582) and ~2,000 below the aggressive May anchor (8,082). April is pacing roughly flat to March (6,304, ~4% below). No Glasswing inflection is visible in the feed yet. Counter-argument: NVD publishing latency is typically 4–8 weeks from discovery to publish, so Glasswing-attributable CVEs from the April 7 launch largely haven't surfaced yet. Expect the first meaningful signal in May–June 2026, with fuller visibility after Anthropic's planned July disclosure.

counter_arguments: CVE volume has NOT spiked post-Glasswing launch (April 7) through day 18: April is extrapolating to ~6,082, roughly flat-to-slightly-below March's 6,304, not the +500 to +5,000/mo scenarios. Two reads: (a) NVD publishing latency is 4–8 weeks so Glasswing discoveries may not appear yet; (b) Glasswing disclosures are being staged through coordinated vendor patches, so they'll arrive lumpily rather than as a smooth monthly trend.
The March 2026 spike to 6,304 (+57.1% YoY vs March 2025) clearly predates Glasswing and is driven by CNA expansion, automated fuzzing, and broader disclosure norms — not AI discovery.

CISA KEV April additions (14 through day 18; zero on 2026-04-17 and 2026-04-18) are slightly below the 2026 monthly average (~23/mo pre-April, but with Jan at 17 this is closer to pacing). No AI-driven KEV inflection visible yet. And KEV additions are editorial decisions by CISA, not purely volume-driven. Five of fourteen April entries are legacy re-adds (2009, 2012, 2020, 2023 CVEs) that reflect CISA catalog housekeeping, not new exploitation.

Microsoft's 163-CVE April Patch Tuesday is notable, but the October 2025 spike of 167 occurred pre-Glasswing, so high-volume Patch Tuesdays are not novel. Microsoft operates on a lumpy cadence; one data point does not establish a trend.

Weekly CVE count for W16 partial (Apr 13–18, 6 days) is 1,143 — a daily pace of ~190. W15 full week (Apr 6–12) was 1,615 = ~231/day; W14 (Mar 30–Apr 5) was 1,304 = ~186/day. The post-Glasswing weeks are within normal weekly variance. No clear inflection.
errors: stack.watch RHEL8 version-specific /8.0 path returned 404 — RHEL 8 April count retained from prior baseline (23). Parent path at /redhat/enterprise-linux still works for YTD but does not split by version.

Prior run committed dashboard updates in commit 0e8b6a2, but subsequent commits c7572b1 (Exploit Watch List) and b3cbc30 (CVSS column) inadvertently reverted the Mythos data in docs/dashboard.html and docs/index.html to pre-refresh values. This run re-applies the refresh on top of the latest commits.
